'''Aaron Murphy''' is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], as a {{rlp|fb|wg|ce}} or {{rlp|sr}}.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/player/0,25470,12208_375281,00.html |title=Aaron Murphy |work=Sky Sports |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100818200947/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/player/0%2C25470%2C12208_375281%2C00.html |archivedate=2010-08-18 }}</ref>

He previously played for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].

==Background==
Murphy was born in [[Leeds]], West Yorkshire, England.

==Career==
He commenced his [[Super League]] career with [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats|Wakefield Trinity]] in 2008. In 2010, after an injury to the Wakefield Trinity first choice fullback [[Matt Blaymire]], Murphy managed to hold down a regular first team place. In 2012 Murphy moved to the [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] side where he became an integral part of the team, playing a big part in the clubs [[League Leaders' Shield]] win in 2013 and subsequent play off campaigns.  At the end of the 2020 season he signed a two-year deal with [[RFL Championship]] side [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18682201.bradford-bulls-sign-huddersfields-murphy-two-year-deal/|title=Bradford Bulls sign Huddersfield's Murphy on two-year deal |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=28 August 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref>
